descriptions.title: Use this slide as the opening title page for the presentation, containing five text boxes for the main title, company name, subtitle, and preparation details.
descriptions.agenda: This slide serves as the agenda or table of contents, with one text box for the title.
descriptions.concept: Use this slide to introduce the main problem being addressed, with two text boxes for the title and problem description.
descriptions.section_divider: This slide acts as a section divider for research results, with one text box for the title.
descriptions.section_divider_2: This slide serves as a section divider for competitive analysis, with one text box for the title.
descriptions.comparison: Use this slide to compare competitors, featuring three text boxes for titles and subtitles, and one image placeholder for visual data.
descriptions.bullets: This slide highlights interview insights, with five text boxes for titles and subtitles.
descriptions.bullets_2: Use this slide to describe the target market, with seven subtitle text boxes for different market segments.
descriptions.process: This slide outlines the user flow, with multiple text boxes for titles and subtitles describing the process steps.
descriptions.summary: Use this slide to summarize key learnings, with two text boxes for the title and summary content.
descriptions.gallery: This slide showcases advertising and product samples, with one title text box and three image placeholders.
descriptions.section_divider_3: This slide acts as a section divider for the completed moodboard, with one text box for the title.
descriptions.closing: Use this slide for closing with contact information, featuring two text boxes for the title and contact details.
